# GovZempic

GovZempic is an interactive civic tool that lets users simulate cutting or slimming US, Polish, and Japanese government budget programs — and see the resulting Universal Basic Income (UBI) monthly dividend per citizen.

## What it does

- Users can "kill" (fully cut) or "slim" (50% cut) any government spending program
- 50% of total savings are redistributed equally to all citizens as a UBI dividend
- Supports USA, Poland, and Japan with real budget data (FY2026 CBO for US, 2024 data for PL/JP)
- Includes preset scenarios (e.g. "Libertarian Gov" kills all welfare/entitlements, slims military)
- Shows household income projections based on family size
- Projects UBI growth over 5–20 years using GDP growth rate
- Calculates debt-free year based on annual savings and economic growth compounding

## Data sources

- USA: CBO Budget and Economic Outlook FY2026, enacted appropriations
- Poland: Ministry of Finance Budget Act 2024, ZUS Annual Report 2024, NFZ Financial Plan 2024
- Japan: Ministry of Finance FY2024 Budget (令和6年度予算)

## Key assumptions

- 50% of program savings are distributed as UBI (remaining 50% reduces deficit/debt)
- UBI is split equally across the full adult+child population of each country
- "Slim" = 50% budget reduction; "Kill" = 100% elimination
- Growth projections use compound annual GDP growth applied to UBI base

## Tech stack

Next.js 16 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion

## Purpose

Educational and satirical political tool. Not affiliated with any government or political party.
